title: Writing: Expressing Wants and Needs
learningGoal: Construct grammatically accurate sentences expressing desire using dative pronouns and matching adjectives.
instructions: In this writing task, you must completely forget English sentence structure. You are writing in the 'To me [object] is needed' format. Pay attention to the gender of the objects when choosing adjective endings.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi:
1. I want hot tea. (Tea is feminine)
2. What do you want? (Use formal 'Aapko')
3. I want a big house. (House is masculine)
4. I want cold water too. (Water is masculine, Use 'bhi' for too)
promptGoal: Applying the 'chahiye' construction and adjective agreement in written form.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: मुझे गरम चाय चाहिए। आपको क्या चाहिए? मुझे एक बड़ा घर चाहिए। मुझे ठंडा पानी भी चाहिए।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Mujhe garam chai chahiye. Aapko kya chahiye? Mujhe ek bada ghar chahiye. Mujhe thanda paani bhi chahiye.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I want hot tea. What do you want? I want a big house. I want cold water too.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect. 'Mujhe' and 'Aapko' are used instead of 'Main' and 'Aap'. 'Bhi' is placed immediately after 'paani'.

evaluationCriteria.description: Must use 'Mujhe' and 'Aapko'. Using 'Main' or 'Aap' results in failure.
The word 'chahiye' must be at the very end of the sentence.
Must use 'thanda' (masculine) for water, and 'bada' (masculine) for house.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: मुझे गरम चाय चाहिए। आपको क्या चाहिए? मुझे एक बड़ा घर चाहिए। मुझे ठंडा पानी भी चाहिए।
acceptableAlternates: Mujhe garam chai chahiye. Aapko kya chahiye? Mujhe ek bada ghar chahiye. Mujhe thanda paani bhi chahiye.
mujhe garam chai chahiye. aapko kya chahiye? mujhe bada ghar chahiye. mujhe thanda paani bhi chahiye.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check against subject pronouns.

criteria.name: Pronoun Error Check
criteria.description: If 'Main' or 'Aap' is used with 'chahiye', the score is 0.

feedbackRules.onPass: Incredible! You have mastered the most difficult grammatical concept for English speakers.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 2 and 3. You CANNOT say 'Main chahiye'. You must say 'Mujhe chahiye' (To me it is needed).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Check your placement of 'bhi' (too/also). It should go right after the thing you also want.

hintText: Read the sentences back to yourself. Does 'Chahiye' come at the very end of every sentence? It should.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Mujhe chahiye garam chai' (Using English word order).
